THE DESIGNER IS CREATING A POSTER WITH EIGHT CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM. EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. TO CALCULATE THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL EIGHT CIRCLES, WE USE THE FORMULA FOR THE AREA OF A CIRCLE, A = πR². THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS R, THE SECOND IS 1.1R, THE THIRD IS (1.1)²R, AND SO ON UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE, WHICH HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)⁷R. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF ALL EIGHT CIRCLES. THIS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + (1.1)⁶ + (1.1)⁸ + (1.1)¹⁰ + (1.1)¹² + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS SERIES CAN BE SIMPLIFIED USING THE GEOMETRIC SERIES FORMULA. THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL EIGHT CIRCLES IS πR² * [((1.1)¹⁶ - 1)/( (1.1)² - 1 )]. THIS IS THE REQUIRED EXPRESSION IN TERMS OF R AND π.

THE FIFTH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)⁴R. ITS CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2π*(1.1)⁴R. THIS CIRCUMFERENCE IS USED TO PLACE TEXT CHARACTERS WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED, WE DIVIDE THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE SUM OF THE WIDTH OF EACH CHARACTER AND THE GAP. ASSUMING EACH CHARACTER TAKES UP 1 CM OF SPACE, THE TOTAL SPACE REQUIRED PER CHARACTER IS 1.5 CM. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE IS 2π*(1.1)⁴R. DIVIDING THIS BY 1.5 GIVES THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS. THIS MUST BE AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE C
